sample_themes.name: Extraction and analysis as separate systems
sample_themes.description: Most pipelines couple them; this repo extracts ALL metadata once then runs arbitrary LLM prompts on extracted data.

sample_themes.name: Toggleable per-frame ML feature extraction
sample_themes.description: Each ML feature (labels, text, celebrity, moderation, captioning) is independently enabled — extensibility via config.

sample_themes.name: LLM-friendly extraction as first principle
sample_themes.description: Every design flows from the insight that LLMs need structured clean content; 55+ extraction strategies because content types differ.

sample_themes.name: Tiered fetch fallback chain as failure-mode knowledge graph
sample_themes.description: Firestore cache → curl-impersonate → proxy retry → Puppeteer (with sideLoad hint) → stale-cache fallback; each layer maps to a specific web-fetch failure.

sample_themes.name: Caption-first as primary extraction layer
sample_themes.description: ~80% of YouTube videos already have captions — fetching them is instant, free, timestamped. Whisper/Transcribe are fallbacks, not defaults.

sample_themes.name: Adopt-don't-rebuild conclusion
sample_themes.description: 8 years + 399 commits of YouTube-API maintenance burden argue against replicating; first explicit 'install the package, don't extract patterns' recommendation in the series.

sample_evidence.quote: extraction and analysis are separate problems that should be separate systems. Most video processing pipelines couple them
Per-frame ML feature extraction (extr-srv-image-extraction.py): Each Rekognition API call (labels, text, celebrity, moderation) is independently toggleable
the web crawling problem has bifurcated. There are crawlers built for humans (Scrapy, Beautiful Soup) and crawlers built for machines (crawl4ai)
fetch a URL is not one problem — it's a chain of failure modes. CrawlerHost.cachedScrap() in src/api/crawler.ts:1374 lines runs a layered strategy

sample_themes.name: 3-tier resource pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD)
sample_themes.description: Browser pool with warmth tiers and janitor lifecycle — claims 10x memory reduction. Pattern portable beyond browsers.

sample_themes.name: Hook lifecycle with 8 ordered hooks and security model
sample_themes.description: 8 hooks with explicit sequence and per-hook guidance; CVE fixes tightened restricted builtins, URL schemes, deserialization allowlists.

sample_themes.description: 10 hook types (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, SearchProvider, etc.) with priority ordering, auto-discovery, plugin.json manifests.

sample_themes.name: YouTube → audio → transcript lifecycle
sample_themes.description: pytubefix download + moviepy audio extract + Transcribe/Whisper async lifecycle in ~30 lines per handler.

sample_themes.name: Plugin system via pluggy (setuptools entrypoints)
sample_themes.description: Third-party OCR/layout/table model registration without forking. Most sophisticated extension mechanism in the space.

sample_themes.name: Validation without maintenance still stagnates
sample_themes.description: Automation ensures format integrity but not content freshness or link liveness; lifecycle mgmt missing.

sample_evidence.quote: The 3-tier browser pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD) in the Docker server is production infrastructure thinking. PERMANENT browsers stay warm for frequent clients

The 10 hook types in server/utils/PluginManager.ts (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, etc.) with priority-based ordering and auto-discovery is a clean extensibility model
Uses `pytubefix` for download + `moviepy` for audio extraction to MP3. This is the exact YouTube content pipeline T27 needs.

sample_themes.name: Testing absence as reference-architecture habit
sample_themes.description: 52 Python files, 1 test file — AWS reference samples routinely skip tests.

sample_themes.name: Zero tests compensated by traffic-volume observability
sample_themes.description: Production 10k-star service ships no tests — quality floor is TypeScript strict + lint + Cloud Logging. Works for originator, fails for adopters.

sample_themes.name: Integration tests through real transport
sample_themes.description: TestServer starts a real Koa HTTP server on an ephemeral port; tests exercise actual middleware, routing, serialization — not mocks.

sample_themes.name: Structural validation prevents format decay
sample_themes.description: format.py + links.py + tests + three CI workflows enforce `API|Description|Auth|HTTPS|CORS` schema.

sample_themes.name: Validation without maintenance still stagnates
sample_themes.description: Automation ensures format integrity but not content freshness or link liveness; lifecycle mgmt missing.

sample_themes.name: Golden-file snapshot testing for extraction
sample_themes.description: 40+ connector types tested via expected markdown comparison — regression model for T28.

sample_evidence.quote: No tests. The tests/ directory has 1 file. Don't follow this pattern.
Zero automated tests. No .test.ts, no .spec.ts, no describe(), nothing. firebase-functions-test sits in devDependencies but is never imported

TestServer Pattern -- MEDIUM. server/test/TestServer.ts creates a real Koa HTTP server on an ephemeral port for integration tests. SoNash tests mock httpsCallable -- the Outline approach tests the actual transport
This is a curated list that invested in structural integrity tooling

sample_themes.name: IP blocking as central operational challenge

sample_themes.name: Adversarial infrastructure design
sample_themes.description: Every design decision assumes the upstream (Twitter) will try to block you; session pooling, rate limit tracking, parser versioning.

sample_themes.name: Per-endpoint preemptive rate limiting
sample_themes.description: Track rate limits per endpoint with preemptive backoff — more sophisticated than post-429 handling.

sample_themes.name: Adversarial dependency as existential risk
sample_themes.description: 12.8K stars but effectively dead — Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. Never build on a hostile foundation.

sample_themes.name: Adversarial-input security posture for AI agents
sample_themes.description: SECURITY.md explicitly names the threat: agents autonomously constructing commands from untrusted input. Codec allowlists + never-shell=True.

sample_themes.name: HTTP service without default hardening
sample_themes.description: mineru-api has no auth, no rate limit, no upload cap, no CORS/TrustedHost, FastAPI docs enabled. Anti-pattern SoNash security-auditor exists to prevent.

sample_evidence.quote: IP blocking is the real engineering problem. The most sophisticated code in this library isn't the transcript fetching -- it's the proxy infrastructure
Nitter understands adversarial infrastructure -- how to build a service that works against the interests of its data source.
limiting before hitting 429, not after
Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. The project has 12.8K stars but is effectively dead.

top_candidates.description: Transcribe handler polls with time.sleep(30) in a blocking while loop. Acceptable for CLI, but must not be copied into agent pipelines. Use async callbacks or event-driven patterns instead.

top_candidates.name: Backend + Pipeline separation pattern
top_candidates.description: Backends handle format-specific parsing, pipelines orchestrate processing stages. T28 should separate source extraction from analysis orchestration.

top_candidates.name: program.md Agent Instruction Pattern
top_candidates.description: 114-line autonomous research protocol: setup, experiment loop, keep/discard, crash recovery, NEVER STOP, simplicity criterion, TSV logging. Compare against SKILL.md.

top_candidates.name: Frame sampling + smart dedup pipeline
top_candidates.description: moviepy frame extraction with configurable FPS + FAISS/OpenSearch similarity dedup. Portable pattern for local video processing.

top_candidates.name: Multi-granularity hierarchy (frame → shot → scene)
top_candidates.description: Three-level temporal decomposition. Each level has summaries, timestamps, metadata. Structures how video content is analyzed.

top_candidates.name: Toggleable per-frame ML feature extraction
top_candidates.description: Each ML feature independently enabled. Config-driven feature selection. Extensibility pattern for adding new capabilities.

top_candidates.name: Subtitle-to-frame timestamp alignment
top_candidates.description: Map Transcribe subtitle segments to frame timestamps. Non-obvious engineering for multimodal analysis.

top_candidates.name: OpenSearch dependency for similarity (anti-pattern)
top_candidates.description: FAISS alternative exists in same repo. Don't add search cluster for video dedup.

top_candidates.name: Multi-provider fallback generator (iterProviders)
top_candidates.description: Generator yields provider clients in preference order; for/of catches per-provider errors and advances. Small port (~40 lines). Reuse across deep-research, multi-SERP, transcript providers, embedding providers.

top_candidates.name: youtube-transcript-api as T27 primary extraction layer
top_candidates.description: pip install youtube-transcript-api. One-line caption fetch with language selection, translation, SRT/WebVTT output. 7K stars, MIT, 100% coverage, 2 deps. First adoption recommendation in analysis series.

top_candidates.name: Innertube API reverse-engineering approach
top_candidates.description: YouTube captions fetched via undocumented /youtubei/v1/player API with ANDROID client context. No API key needed. Fragile but library maintains compatibility across 8 years.

top_candidates.name: Validation without maintenance trap
top_candidates.description: Format validation scripts ensure structural integrity of new entries, but don't ensure entries are current, links are live, or categories are relevant. Missing piece: lifecycle management.

top_candidates.name: No tests (anti-pattern)
top_candidates.description: 52 Python files, 1 test. AWS reference architectures often skip tests. Don't follow this pattern.

top_candidates.name: Commercial OSS without tests as an architectural stance
top_candidates.description: Reader runs at scale with zero tests. Quality floor = TypeScript + lint + observability + traffic volume. Defensible for originator, does not transfer to adopters.

top_candidates.name: Zero tests in a 10k-star production service
top_candidates.description: Reader has no test suite. Quality = TypeScript strict + lint + traffic. Works for originator (observability), fails for adopters. 'No tests' requires compensating monitoring, not the other way around.

top_candidates.name: TestServer ephemeral-port integration testing
top_candidates.description: Custom test utility that starts a real Koa HTTP server on port 0 for integration tests. Tests exercise middleware, routing, and serialization through the actual transport.

top_candidates.name: youtube-transcript-api as T27 primary extraction layer
top_candidates.description: pip install youtube-transcript-api. One-line caption fetch with language selection, translation, SRT/WebVTT output. 7K stars, MIT, 100% coverage, 2 deps. First adoption recommendation in analysis series.

top_candidates.name: IP ban workaround operational guide
top_candidates.description: Production-tested guide to handling YouTube IP blocks: generic proxies, Webshare rotating residential IPs, retry strategies.

top_candidates.name: 100% coverage + 2-dep library packaging
top_candidates.description: Poetry with lock file, py.typed marker, --fail-under=100, only requests + defusedxml runtime deps. Reference for well-packaged Python library.

top_candidates.description: 22 auto-run + 7 ask-first with per-command reasons. Structured encoding of autonomy guardrails. Retrofit candidate for add-debt, pr-review, session-end, audit-* skills.

top_candidates.description: 10 hook types (API, AuthProvider, EmailTemplate, Processor, Task, SearchProvider, etc.). Priority-based ordering. Auto-discovery via glob. plugin.json manifests. Structural client/server/shared split per plugin.

top_candidates.description: skill.py reads SKILL.md from package data, stamps version, writes to ~/.claude/skills/. Directly retires cross_locale_config problem. Needs Node port.

top_candidates.name: Ambient CLAUDE.md PR Workflow
top_candidates.description: 4-step embedded PR workflow (monitor CI, check reviews, fix + reply with SHA, verify mergeStateStatus CLEAN). Alternative to invoked /pr-review.

top_candidates.name: Embedded Task() Subagent Patterns
top_candidates.description: SKILL.md includes literal Task() invocations for agent copy-paste. Reduces cognitive load for invoking subagents.

top_candidates.name: AGENTS.md separation pattern
top_candidates.description: Separate file for Codex/parallel agent guidance instead of embedding in CLAUDE.md. Consider if CLAUDE.md grows further.

top_candidates.description: npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py. Unverified. Could be a real skill registry relevant to JASON-OS distribution.

top_candidates.name: YouTube audio extraction pipeline
top_candidates.description: pytubefix download + moviepy audio extraction to MP3. Full lifecycle in ~30 lines. Reference for T27 YouTube content extraction.

top_candidates.name: Amazon Transcribe async job lifecycle
top_candidates.description: Start transcription job -> poll -> fetch transcript from S3 -> cleanup. Shows speaker labels, language config, S3 output routing.

top_candidates.name: Don't build your own transcript fetcher (anti-pattern)
top_candidates.description: 8 years and 399 commits maintaining compatibility with YouTube's changing API. Use the library, don't replicate the maintenance burden.

top_candidates.name: Blocking poll anti-pattern
top_candidates.description: Transcribe handler polls with time.sleep(30) in a blocking while loop. Acceptable for CLI, but must not be copied into agent pipelines. Use async callbacks or event-driven patterns instead.

top_candidates.name: Graceful dependency checking
top_candidates.description: dependency_exists() checks at runtime, logs warning, falls back. T28 needs this for optional deps (Whisper, Chrome, gh CLI).

top_candidates.name: Monolithic single-file tool anti-pattern
top_candidates.description: 280 lines, no CLI, no config file, hard-coded vars. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate.

top_candidates.name: Hard-coded source config anti-pattern
top_candidates.description: Module-level variables for config including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars. Anti-twelve-factor.

top_candidates.name: MCP server as CLI subcommand (3 transports)
top_candidates.description: MCP server accessed via 'qmd mcp' subcommand. Stdio + HTTP foreground + HTTP daemon with PID file. Daemon avoids expensive model reload.

top_candidates.name: Fixture-based eval harness with difficulty tiers
top_candidates.description: Test queries categorized easy/medium/hard with expected docs. Precision@k, recall, MRR, F1 across 4 backends. 'qmd bench' CLI for reproducible quality measurement.

top_candidates.name: MCP auto-discovery from CLI metadata
top_candidates.description: Zero-schema MCP server that introspects Click CLI commands and auto-generates MCP tool definitions. ~200 lines. Uses CliRunner for execution. Eliminates manual schema duplication.

candidate_buckets.name: MCP as a first-class integration surface

top_candidates.name: Claude Code plugin manifest (marketplace.json)

top_candidates.name: 3-tier resource pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD)
top_candidates.description: Browser pool with tiered warmth and janitor lifecycle. 10x memory claim. Resource pooling for JASON-OS agent pools / MCP connections.

top_candidates.name: x-* request header protocol
top_candidates.description: Runtime config surface via headers (11+ headers). Cleaner than query-string pollution. Applicable when CAS exposes /website-analysis over MCP or as agent-facing tool surface.

top_candidates.name: MCP OAuth scope-filtered tool registration
top_candidates.description: Production MCP server that filters tool registration based on OAuth scopes. Tools only visible if token has matching scope. Uses @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with Zod validation.

top_candidates.name: Command pattern (transport-agnostic business logic)
top_candidates.description: Business logic in transport-agnostic command files (~20 commands). Both HTTP API routes and MCP tools delegate to same commands. Enables adding new surfaces without duplicating logic.

top_candidates.name: buildAPIContext() MCP-to-API bridge
top_candidates.description: Bridge function that constructs a Koa-style APIContext from MCP request context. Enables MCP tools to reuse existing API middleware and command infrastructure without duplication.

top_candidates.name: Presenter response formatting layer
top_candidates.description: JSON response formatting layer between models and API/MCP consumers. Each model has a presenter controlling exposed fields and formatting.

top_candidates.name: docling-mcp as extraction backend
top_candidates.description: MCP server wrapping docling conversion. T28 could use as document extraction backend instead of building one.

top_candidates.name: 3-tier resource pool (PERMANENT/HOT/COLD)
top_candidates.description: Browser pool with tiered warmth and janitor lifecycle. 10x memory claim. Resource pooling for JASON-OS agent pools / MCP connections.

top_candidates.name: Colab notebook: full-site crawling methodology
top_candidates.description: The only practical multi-URL example using r.jina.ai. Useful IF /website-analysis adds a bulk mode later.

top_candidates.name: Open Skills Ecosystem Investigation
top_candidates.description: npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py. Unverified. Could be a real skill registry relevant to JASON-OS distribution.

top_candidates.name: Results TSV Logging Convention
top_candidates.description: commit/val_bpb/memory_gb/status/description. Already have JSONL equivalent.

top_candidates.name: Single-file-everything pattern
top_candidates.description: 390 links in one README.md. Works at small scale, unmaintainable at large scale. Watch for same impulse in EXTRACTIONS.md, MEMORY.md, SKILL_INDEX.md.

top_candidates.name: Async task state machine with TTL cleanup
top_candidates.description: In-memory task dict with four states (pending/processing/completed/failed), asyncio.Queue work channel, asyncio.Semaphore concurrency cap, background sweep for TTL cleanup, FS-backed output artifacts keyed by task ID. Ephemeral state. Answers T28 Gap Agent A Q5 (progressive extraction).

top_candidates.name: Sliding window for long-input memory bounding
top_candidates.description: get_processing_window_size(default=64) applied identically across all three backends. Pages loaded, inferred, freed in window-sized batches. Peak memory strictly bounded.

top_candidates.name: Graph-only persistence without versioning
top_candidates.description: graph.json has no schema_version, no migration path. Format changes break old graphs silently. SoNash analysis.json schema_version is the right approach.

top_candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback chain
top_candidates.description: Pluggable extraction engines coordinated by a fallback-list builder. buildFallbackList → scrapeURLWithEngine → try next or NoEnginesLeftError. Named failure mode, documented order, engine-level telemetry.

top_candidates.name: curl-impersonate + TLS fingerprint concept
top_candidates.description: Understanding that anti-bot defenses fingerprint the full TLS/TCP stack explains why firecrawl/crawl4ai leak through some sites.

top_candidates.name: curl-impersonate as Docker LD_PRELOAD base layer
top_candidates.description: Two-stage Docker build: FROM lwthiker/curl-impersonate -> FROM node:22. Copy .so + LD_PRELOAD. Template for any future self-hosted fetcher that needs to defeat anti-bot TLS fingerprinting.

top_candidates.name: Proxy rotation infrastructure (Webshare)
top_candidates.description: ProxyConfig ABC -> GenericProxyConfig, WebshareProxyConfig. Rotating residential IPs, retry-on-429, connection-close for forced rotation.

top_candidates.name: Session pool with per-endpoint rate limiting
top_candidates.description: Multiple sessions rotate on rate limits. Per-session per-endpoint tracking with preemptive backoff.

top_candidates.name: ADVERSARIAL_DEPENDENCY
top_candidates.description: Building product on scraping hostile platform. Twitter killed nitter by changing APIs. 12.8K stars, effectively dead.

top_candidates.name: Multi-agent orchestration via openai_swarm
top_candidates.description: Reference implementation of multi-agent coordination around a single scraping task. Worth reading to inform SoNash agent teams and JASON-OS agent boundary design.

top_candidates.name: Deep Research API usage pattern
top_candidates.description: How firecrawl's Deep Research API is invoked in practice (apartment-finder). Interactive preference gathering → research call → LLM analysis → structured JSON.

top_candidates.name: Whisper tuned params (beam_size=10, vad_filter)
top_candidates.description: beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True to skip silence and prevent Whisper hallucination-on-silence issue.

top_candidates.name: 58-example cookbook pattern
top_candidates.description: The examples/ directory as product-marketing-in-code. 58 self-contained apps, each pairing firecrawl with a specific LLM or framework.

top_candidates.name: init()-based plugin registry with domain routing
top_candidates.description: Single-method Extractor interface + global map + init() auto-registration + blank imports + universal fallback. 46 extractors, zero boilerplate. Go-specific but principle universal.

top_candidates.name: Hallucinated dependencies (slopsquatting)
top_candidates.description: AI suggests non-existent packages. Verify against registries, use SBOM, use Dep-Hallucinator tool.

top_candidates.name: Context momentum / hallucinated logic
top_candidates.description: Early AI misinterpretations steer wrong. Clear context periodically, use separate threads per phase.

top_candidates.name: Structural entropy (code bloat)
top_candidates.description: AI generates verbose, redundant code. Act as ruthless editor. Use git diffs, static analysis.

top_candidates.name: 15-entry checkpoint cycle pattern
top_candidates.description: Periodic maintenance rhythm during long operations. Every N items: rebuild indexes, audit quality, check for anti-patterns. Mid-session companion to session-end.

top_candidates.name: Hook lifecycle with 8 ordered hooks
top_candidates.description: 8 lifecycle hooks with clear sequence and security model. Combine with ArchiveBox governance for JASON-OS hook ecosystem.

top_candidates.name: No-license-on-purpose trap
top_candidates.description: Karpathy can get away with no license because he's Karpathy. 9,611 forks in legal ambiguity. Don't follow this example for JASON-OS.

top_candidates.description: GPL-3.0 copyleft blocks adoption as dependency. Must extract patterns only, never import code.
